articles.title: Explained: Why Oracle shares crashed 10% in extended trading despite earnings beat
articles.description: Oracle shares fell sharply in after-hours trading despite beating earnings estimates, as investors reacted to aggressive AI spending plans and rising debt concerns. The company’s massive capital raise and infrastructure push raised fears of over-leverage, even as strong revenue growth and bullish guidance highlighted its expanding role in AI cloud infrastructure.

articles.title: SC calls homemakers as 'nation builders', sets pay
articles.description: The Supreme Court of India has declared homemakers as nation builders. A monthly compensation of Rs. 30,000 will now be awarded for the loss of domestic and caregiving services. The court acknowledged the economic value of unpaid household work. This ruling recognizes the significant contributions of homemakers to families and society.

articles.title: Tamil Nadu to setup special monitoring team for uninterrupted power supply across the State
articles.description: Tamil Nadu's power distribution body, TNPDCL, is forming special teams in all districts. This move aims to fix faults quickly and reduce power cuts. The state serves over 35 million consumers. Special funds are allocated for urgent repairs. Electricity Minister C T R Nirmal Kumar reviewed measures to ensure continuous power for urban and suburban residents.

articles.title: No individual data, Sebi to tweak AMC exec pay disclosure norms

articles.description: In May, Indian businesses tightened their belts when it came to overseas investments, marking a notable decline from April's figures. A drop in guarantees, equity investments, and loans contributed to this trend.

articles.title: Adani Energy Solutions’ IntelliSmart deal seen a costly but timely bet
articles.description: In a bold strategic move, Adani Energy Solutions has secured the smart metering firm IntelliSmart Infrastructure for a staggering ₹3,050 crore. This acquisition is not just a financial transaction; it's a calculated step to amplify their presence in the burgeoning smart meter market. With the government gearing up for a comprehensive deployment of smart meters, the timing couldn't be better.

articles.title: ₹42-cr. fine slapped for stone quarry violation
articles.description: The Department of Mines has slapped a quarry operator with a hefty fine of ₹42 crore for quarrying 40,000 cubic metres of stones in excess of the permissible limit. Sources said the department officials conducted an inspection in a stone quarry at Singaththakurichi in Srivaikundam taluk a few months ago following complaints of excess mining of stones.

articles.title: Mumbai’s Biggest Traffic Experiment Begins
articles.description: Mumbai’s Bandra-Kurla Complex (BKC) will observe a ‘Public Transport Day’ every Friday starting June 12, as part of a new initiative to reduce traffic congestion and encourage the use of public transport. The move has been introduced by MMRDA in collaboration with transport authorities and major offices in the business district. Employees working in BKC are being encouraged to avoid using private cars and two-wheelers and instead travel by metro, local trains, buses, or shared transport. Authorities say nearly two lakh people commute to BKC daily, and the initiative aims to ease traffic pressure, improve mobility, and reduce vehicle emissions in the area.
